gameplay-and-new-features.name: Rayman Legends Retold Gameplay and New Features

layout.desktop: Three-column feature card grid with short descriptions and player value notes.
layout.mobile: Single-column feature cards with compact labels.

gameplay-and-new-features.title: Rayman Legends Retold Gameplay and New Features
gameplay-and-new-features.subtitle: Rayman Legends Retold is a bold 3D reimagining of the 2013 platformer, not just a simple port. It adds new story content, new stages, voiced cinematics, dragon rides, and updated co-op features.
gameplay-and-new-features.intro: This module explains what is new in Rayman Legends Retold and how the remake changes the original fast-paced platforming formula while keeping Rayman’s core movement, rhythm stages, co-op chaos, and collectible-focused level design.

items.title: Expanded 3D Reimagining
items.description: Rayman Legends Retold rebuilds the classic side-scrolling platformer with stunning 3D visuals, camera movement, and more cinematic presentation.
items.player_value: Good for players who want the familiar Rayman flow with a modern visual style.

items.title: New Retold Story
items.description: A shadowy villain spreads corruption across the Glade of Dreams, sending Rayman, Globox, Barbara, Grand Minimus, and Murphy on a new adventure.
items.player_value: Gives returning players a fresh reason to replay the world instead of only revisiting old levels.

items.title: Fully Voiced Cinematics
items.description: The game adds voiced cinematics and fully voiced characters, giving the story and cast a more expressive style.
items.player_value: Makes the adventure easier to follow for new players and more lively for co-op groups.

items.title: Sixth Realm and Returning Levels
items.description: A rich selection of iconic levels returns alongside a mysterious new sixth realm filled with hidden Teensies and new surprises.
items.player_value: Adds new exploration goals while keeping the collectible-heavy structure fans expect.

items.title: Four New Musical Stages
items.description: New musical stages ask players to time movement with the beat while running, jumping, gliding, and punching through hazards.
items.player_value: Best for players who enjoy rhythm-based platforming and replaying stages for cleaner runs.

items.title: Dragon Rides and Cave of Trials
items.description: Rayman Legends Retold adds epic dragon rides and Cave of Trials challenges that push movement timing and reaction speed.
items.player_value: Adds higher-skill replay content beyond normal story progression.

items.title: Up to 4-Player Couch Co-op
items.description: The full adventure supports solo play or up to four players in couch co-op, with players racing for Lums and competing to rescue Teensies.
items.player_value: Ideal for family play, local co-op sessions, and chaotic party-style platforming.

items.title: Improved Kung Foot
items.description: Kung Foot returns with improved controls, power-ups, and customizable rules for local competitive play.
items.player_value: Adds a quick side mode for groups who want fast matches outside the main adventure.

cta.label: Open Rayman Legends Retold Gameplay and New Features

beginner-guide.name: Rayman Legends Retold Beginner Guide

layout.desktop: Vertical guide timeline with step number, action, and practical tip.
layout.mobile: Compact numbered steps with one tip per card.

beginner-guide.title: Rayman Legends Retold Beginner Guide
beginner-guide.subtitle: Start with the basics: move cleanly, collect Lums, rescue Teensies, replay stages, and use co-op without losing focus. The game is built for solo players and up to 4-player couch co-op.
beginner-guide.intro: Rayman Legends Retold rewards simple movement done well. New players should focus on smooth jumps, smart gliding, clear attacks, hidden Teensies, and replaying stages to improve completion before taking on harder challenges like musical stages and the Cave of Trials.

items.title: Learn the Core Movement First
items.action: Practice running, jumping, gliding, and punching before chasing every collectible.
items.tip: Rayman levels move fast, so clean movement matters more than rushing on your first run.

items.title: Use Punching to Clear Space
items.action: Punch enemies and obstacles when a path gets crowded.
items.tip: Attacking early keeps your jump path open and helps avoid losing rhythm in busy sections.

items.title: Collect Lums on the Main Route
items.action: Follow the obvious trail of Lums during your first clear, then replay for missed ones.
items.tip: Lums guide the safest path through many sections and are useful for completion-focused runs.

items.title: Search for Hidden Teensies
items.action: Check side paths, suspicious gaps, and alternate routes instead of only running straight to the exit.
items.tip: Teensies are a major completion target, and many are placed slightly off the main route.

items.title: Replay Levels for Better Completion
items.action: After finishing a stage, replay it to collect missed Lums and rescue missed Teensies.
items.tip: Replays are the easiest way to learn enemy timing, hidden routes, and cleaner movement.

items.title: Treat Musical Stages Like Rhythm Challenges
items.action: Move with the beat and avoid random button mashing during music-based levels.
items.tip: The new musical stages reward timing, so listening to the soundtrack helps as much as watching the screen.

items.title: Use Co-op Roles in 4-Player Sessions
items.action: Let one player lead the route while others focus on Lums, enemies, and hidden Teensies.
items.tip: Co-op can get chaotic, so simple roles help the group complete stages instead of knocking each other around.

items.title: Save Cave of Trials for Skill Practice
items.action: Try Cave of Trials challenges after you are comfortable with jumping, gliding, and reaction timing.
items.tip: These challenges are built to test movement skill and are better after learning the basic level flow.

items.title: Play Kung Foot as a Break Mode
items.action: Use Kung Foot when your group wants quick competitive matches instead of story progress.
items.tip: Improved controls, power-ups, and custom rules make it a good party mode between adventure stages.

cta.label: Open Rayman Legends Retold Beginner Guide

walkthrough-and-levels.name: Rayman Legends Retold Walkthrough and Levels

layout.desktop: Two-column walkthrough timeline with step cards and collectible goals
layout.mobile: Single-column vertical steps with compact goal labels

walkthrough-and-levels.title: Rayman Legends Retold Walkthrough and Level Flow
walkthrough-and-levels.subtitle: Follow the revealed adventure structure: the reimagined Glade of Dreams, returning levels, the sixth realm, musical stages, dragon rides, and Cave of Trials challenges.
walkthrough-and-levels.intro: Rayman Legends Retold rebuilds the 2013 platforming adventure with new 3D visuals, a retold story, fully voiced cinematics, and new gameplay content. Use this module as a clean route map for what players will do first, what to replay for collectibles, and which revealed challenge types matter most.

items.title: Enter the Glade of Dreams
items.details: Begin the adventure as a shadowy villain spreads chaos and corruption across the Glade of Dreams. Rayman and his friends set out to restore peace and rescue Teensies across the realms.
items.player_goal: Learn the basic loop: leap, glide, punch enemies, explore side paths, and watch for collectible routes.

items.title: Use the Level Hub
items.details: The hub connects the opening worlds, replay routes, extra unlocks, and challenge access. Lums return as a currency-style collectible used to unlock extra content such as time trial levels.
items.player_goal: Collect Lums during every run and revisit the hub when new unlocks become available.

items.title: Clear Returning Iconic Levels
items.details: A rich selection of iconic levels returns with new visuals, reworked presentation, and more immersive character interactions. These stages keep the fast side-scrolling platforming focus while adding a more cinematic 3D look.
items.player_goal: Complete each stage once, then replay for missed Teensies, Lums, and cleaner movement routes.

items.title: Explore the Mysterious Sixth Realm
items.details: The remake adds a never-before-seen sixth realm to expand the adventure beyond the original level set. This realm is positioned as one of the key new areas in the retold journey.
items.player_goal: Treat the sixth realm as the major new-world target after learning the returning level rhythm.

items.title: Play the Four New Musical Stages
items.details: Four all-new musical stages join the campaign. These levels are built around movement synced to the beat, turning jumps, attacks, and timing into part of the soundtrack flow.
items.player_goal: Watch the beat, time movement carefully, and replay stages to improve rhythm-based routes.

items.title: Ride Dragons Through Flight Sequences
items.details: Dragon ride stages add flight-focused action with fire breathing, barriers to destroy, pillars to dodge, and Teensies to protect during fast movement sequences.
items.player_goal: Use dragon fire to open the route, dodge hazards early, and keep Teensies safe through the sequence.

items.title: Push Through the Cave of Trials
items.details: The Cave of Trials offers ongoing free challenges that test tighter movement, attacks, and route mastery. These stages are designed for players who want harder replay goals beyond the main adventure path.
items.player_goal: Use trials for skill practice, faster routes, and repeatable post-story challenge progression.

items.title: Replay for Lums, Teensies, and Time Trials
items.details: Replay is built around collecting more Lums, rescuing missed Teensies, unlocking extra content, and improving routes in time trial-style challenges.
items.player_goal: Return to older levels after learning new tricks and aim for cleaner runs instead of rushing only the main path.

cta.label: Open Rayman Legends Retold Walkthrough and Level Flow

characters-and-story.name: Rayman Legends Retold Characters and Story

layout.desktop: Four-column character cards with role, story function, and player-facing notes
layout.mobile: Stacked character cards with short role summaries

characters-and-story.title: Rayman Legends Retold Characters and Story
characters-and-story.subtitle: Meet the confirmed heroes, allies, rescued Teensies, and the new threat behind the retold Glade of Dreams adventure.
characters-and-story.intro: The remake turns Rayman Legends into a more story-driven adventure with fully voiced cinematics, new character interactions, a new villain, and expanded lore for the Glade of Dreams. The core cast follows Rayman, Globox, Barbara, Grand Minimus, and Murphy as they fight to stop corruption across the realms.

items.name: Rayman
items.story_function: Rayman leads the journey to restore peace to the Glade of Dreams after a shadowy villain spreads corruption through the realms.
items.what_players_see: Fast platforming built around leaping, gliding, punching enemies, rescuing Teensies, and exploring colorful worlds.

items.name: Globox
items.story_function: Globox joins Rayman in the retold adventure and is part of the core friend group traveling through the Glade of Dreams.
items.what_players_see: A returning fan-favorite ally featured in the campaign, co-op chaos, and preorder costume content inspired by Rayman 3: Hoodlum Havoc.

items.name: Barbara
items.story_function: Barbara returns as one of Rayman's key companions in the expanded story and voiced cinematic presentation.
items.what_players_see: A heroic teammate included in the main cast and Deluxe Edition Retro Pack costume lineup.

items.name: Grand Minimus
items.story_function: Grand Minimus is part of the confirmed traveling group and connects the adventure to the Teensies that players rescue across levels.
items.what_players_see: A story ally tied closely to Teensie rescue goals and the remake's expanded cast interactions.

items.name: Murphy
items.story_function: Murphy joins the confirmed group on the journey through the Glade of Dreams and supports the retold adventure structure.
items.what_players_see: A returning Rayman companion included in the story setup as the remake expands character interactions.

items.name: Teensies
items.story_function: Teensies are the friends players rescue throughout the Glade of Dreams while clearing worlds and replaying levels.
items.what_players_see: Collectible rescue goals that encourage exploration, replay, and competition in co-op.

items.name: Shadowy Villain
items.story_function: A new villain spreads corruption and chaos through the Glade of Dreams, pushing Rayman and friends into a bigger retold adventure.
items.what_players_see: A new foe tied to the remake's narrative twists, underworld trouble, and expanded world logic.

items.name: Glade of Dreams
items.story_function: The Glade of Dreams is reimagined with new 3D visuals, more immersive worlds, fresh surprises, and a never-before-seen sixth realm.
items.what_players_see: Colorful worlds, returning iconic levels, new realms, musical stages, dragon rides, and Cave of Trials challenges.

cta.label: Open Rayman Legends Retold Characters and Story

co-op-and-kung-foot.name: Rayman Legends Retold Co Op and Kung Foot

layout.desktop: Two-column accordion with multiplayer feature groups
layout.mobile: Single-column accordion optimized for quick platform checks

co-op-and-kung-foot.title: Rayman Legends Retold Co-Op and Kung Foot
co-op-and-kung-foot.subtitle: Play the full adventure in couch co-op, compete for Lums and Teensies, then switch into the upgraded Kung Foot party mode.
co-op-and-kung-foot.intro: Rayman Legends Retold keeps the series' shared-screen chaos at the center of multiplayer. Players can team up through the adventure, race each other for collectibles, knock friends off route, and play the returning Kung Foot mode with improved controls, power-ups, and customizable rules.

items.title: Adventure Couch Co-Op
items.summary: The full adventure supports solo play or up to four-player couch co-op.
content: Play through the campaign together on the same screen.
Jump, glide, punch, and survive the same level routes as a group.
Co-op is designed for fast platforming with shared chaos rather than separate isolated paths.

items.title: Co-Op Goals
items.summary: Players can help each other or compete while clearing stages.
content: Rescue friends and Teensies across levels.
Knock friends off their path to grab collectibles first.
Replay levels to clean up missed Teensies and improve group routes.

items.title: Kung Foot Returns
items.summary: Kung Foot is back as a competitive party mode.
content: Improved controls make the mode easier to read and faster to play.
Customizable rules let players tune the match flow for casual or competitive sessions.

items.title: PC Multiplayer Features
items.summary: Steam and Epic list local and multiplayer support for the PC release.
content: Steam lists Single-player, Shared/Split Screen Co-op, Shared/Split Screen PvP, Shared/Split Screen, Remote Play Together, and Cross-Platform Multiplayer.
Epic Games Store lists Single Player, Local Multiplayer, Multiplayer, and Cross Platform features.
Steam requires a Ubisoft account and supports linking to a Steam account.

items.title: Xbox Multiplayer Notes
items.summary: The Xbox listing confirms local multiplayer for Xbox Series X|S.
content: Xbox lists local multiplayer for 2-4 players.
The Xbox listing states that internet connection, a Ubisoft account, and a Microsoft account are required to access online features.
Xbox editions include the base game together with Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition.

pc-requirements-and-languages.name: Rayman Legends Retold PC Requirements and Languages

layout.desktop: Full-width comparison table with requirement, account, DRM, and language rows
layout.mobile: Stacked spec rows with label and value blocks

pc-requirements-and-languages.title: Rayman Legends Retold PC Requirements and Languages
pc-requirements-and-languages.subtitle: Check PC specs, supported languages, account requirements, and store-specific notes before playing on Steam or Epic Games Store.
pc-requirements-and-languages.intro: Rayman Legends Retold launches on PC through Ubisoft Store, Steam, and Epic Games Store. The PC listings cover Windows requirements, supported languages, account requirements, and Steam DRM details for players who want to prepare their setup early.

items.label: Minimum PC Specs
items.value: Windows 10 64-bit, Intel Core i3-7100 3.9 GHz or AMD Ryzen 3 1200 3.1 GHz, 8 GB RAM, NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 4GB or AMD Radeon RX 5500 XT 4GB, DirectX 9.0, 10 GB available storage, 8 GB dual-channel RAM setup.

items.label: Minimum PC Specs
items.value: Windows 10 64-bit or Windows 11, Intel i3-8100 or Ryzen 3 3100, 16 GB memory, Nvidia GTX 1650 4GB or AMD RX 5500 XT 4GB or Intel Arc A580 8GB, DirectX 12.

items.label: Recommended PC Specs
items.value: Windows 10 64-bit or Windows 11, Intel i5-8400 6c6t or Ryzen 5 3600X, 16 GB memory, Nvidia RTX 2060 6GB or AMD RX 5600 XT 6GB or Intel Arc A750 8GB, DirectX 12.

items.label: Steam Account and DRM
items.value: Ubisoft account required with Steam account linking support. Steam lists Denuvo Anti-Tamper and a 5-per-day machine activation limit.

items.label: Full Audio Languages
items.value: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Portuguese - Brazil, Japanese, Simplified Chinese.

items.label: Interface and Subtitle Languages
items.value: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Dutch, Japanese, Polish, Portuguese - Brazil, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese.

items.label: PC Store Availability
items.value: PC release is available through Ubisoft Store, Steam, and Epic Games Store, with Rayman Origins: Enhanced Edition included in the base package.

cta.label: Open Rayman Legends Retold PC Requirements and Languages

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Key release, platform, and co-op answers for Rayman Legends Retold.

questions.question: When does Rayman Legends Retold release?
questions.answer: Rayman Legends Retold is scheduled to launch on October 1, 2026.

questions.question: Which platforms are confirmed?
questions.answer: Ubisoft has confirmed PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Nintendo Switch 2, PC storefronts such as Steam and Epic Games Store, plus GeForce NOW and Blacknut.

questions.question: Does Rayman Legends Retold support co-op?
questions.answer: Yes. The full adventure supports local couch co-op for up to four players, and Kung Foot returns as a separate party mode.

questions.question: What editions are available?
questions.answer: The announced lineup includes Standard Edition, Deluxe Edition, Launch Edition, and a Deluxe Upgrade option, with the Hoodlum Havoc Pack tied to pre-orders.

questions.question: Is this a remake or a remaster?
questions.answer: Ubisoft is presenting Rayman Legends Retold as a broader reimagining with 3D visuals, new story content, a sixth realm, and new stage types rather than a simple remaster.

questions.question: What does the PC version require?
questions.answer: Current PC store listings point to Windows 10 or 11, a modern mid-range CPU and GPU, 16 GB of memory, and a Ubisoft account requirement on supported PC storefronts.
